# Meridian Osteopathy — Full Content

> Meridian Osteopathy is a multidisciplinary clinic in Christchurch, New Zealand, offering osteopathy, acupuncture, and herbal medicine. We are ACC registered — no GP referral is required.

## Clinic Details

- Name: Meridian Osteopathy
- Location: 21 Coppell Place, Hillmorton, Christchurch 8025, New Zealand
- Phone: 021 086 55151
- Email: info@meridianosteopathy.co.nz
- Website: https://meridianosteopathy.co.nz
- Hours: Mon–Fri 9:00am–7:00pm, Sat 9:00am–1:00pm, Sun closed
- Area served: Christchurch, Canterbury, New Zealand
- Pricing: ACC consultations $80–$85; private (non-ACC) consultations $115–$120
- Booking: https://meridianosteopathy.co.nz/book/

## Services

### Osteopathy

Osteopathy is a regulated allied health profession specialising in musculoskeletal health. Our osteopaths treat a wide range of conditions including back and neck pain, headaches and migraines, joint pain and arthritis, posture imbalances, muscle tightness, pregnancy-related pain, and growth pain — for patients of all ages, from infants to seniors.

Specialties:

- **Paediatric Osteopathy** — Osteopathy is safe and effective for babies, toddlers, and children of all ages. Using gentle, non-invasive techniques, we can help with birth-related discomforts, colic, feeding difficulties, sleep disturbances, growing pains, and postural development. Early treatment supports healthy growth and addresses issues before they become chronic.
- **Pregnancy Related Pain** — Pregnancy places significant demands on the body as it adapts to support new life. Osteopathic treatment can relieve lower back and pelvic pain, sciatica, rib discomfort, and postural strain — with a gentle approach that is safe throughout all trimesters and supports both mother and baby's wellbeing.
- **Elderly Patients** — As we age, joint stiffness, arthritis, reduced mobility and balance issues can affect quality of life. Osteopathic care for older adults uses gentle techniques to improve movement, reduce pain, and support independence — helping manage chronic conditions and maintain an active, fulfilling lifestyle.
- **Sport Injury** — Whether you're a weekend warrior or a competitive athlete, osteopathy can help you recover faster and prevent future injuries. We treat sprains, strains, shoulder and knee pain, and overuse injuries — combining hands-on treatment with personalised rehabilitation advice to get you back to doing what you love.

### Acupuncture

Acupuncture at Meridian Osteopathy blends Traditional Chinese Medicine with modern evidence-based practice. Our practitioners treat a wide range of conditions including chronic pain, headaches, stress and anxiety, sleep difficulties, women's health, digestive concerns, and fatigue — supporting your body's natural ability to heal and restore balance.

Specialties:

- **Pain Relief & Recovery** — Acupuncture is highly effective for both acute and chronic pain. By stimulating specific points on the body, it triggers the release of natural pain-relieving chemicals, reduces inflammation, and improves circulation. Whether you're dealing with back pain, joint issues, headaches, or sports injuries, acupuncture offers a drug-free pathway to lasting relief.
- **Stress, Anxiety & Sleep** — In today's fast-paced world, stress and poor sleep take a significant toll on health. Acupuncture helps regulate the nervous system, lower cortisol levels, and promote a deep sense of calm. Regular treatment can improve sleep quality, reduce anxiety, and restore emotional balance — helping you feel grounded and resilient.
- **Women's Health** — Acupuncture has been used for centuries to support women's health across all life stages. From regulating menstrual cycles and easing period pain to managing pregnancy discomforts and navigating perimenopause and menopause, our practitioners provide gentle, holistic care tailored to your unique needs. Infertility acupuncture is one of our most sought-after specialties — research supports its role in improving ovarian function, regulating hormonal cycles, reducing stress, and enhancing uterine lining receptivity. Whether you are trying to conceive naturally or are undergoing IVF or other assisted reproductive treatments, acupuncture can be a powerful complement to your fertility journey, supporting both physical and emotional wellbeing throughout the process.
- **Digestive & Immune Health** — A healthy gut is the foundation of overall wellbeing. Acupuncture can help regulate digestive function, reduce bloating and discomfort, and support conditions such as IBS, reflux, and nausea. It also plays a role in strengthening immune resilience and managing fatigue, helping your body maintain its natural balance.

### Herbal Medicine

Herbal medicine at Meridian Osteopathy draws on thousands of years of Traditional Chinese Medicine wisdom, combined with contemporary research. Our practitioners create personalised herbal prescriptions using carefully selected plant-based ingredients to address a wide range of conditions — from pain and fatigue to digestive health, hormonal imbalance, and mental wellbeing.

Specialties:

- **Pain & Inflammation** — Herbal formulas have long been used to address pain and reduce inflammation throughout the body. Our practitioners select specific herbs known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, creating personalised blends that work alongside your other treatments to provide sustained relief from chronic pain, arthritis, and musculoskeletal conditions.
- **Stress, Sleep & Mental Wellbeing** — Adaptogenic and calming herbs have a profound ability to support the nervous system, reduce the impact of stress, and promote restful sleep. Our herbal prescriptions are tailored to your individual pattern — whether you're experiencing burnout, anxiety, insomnia, or low mood — to help restore your energy and emotional balance.
- **Digestive Health** — Digestive health is central to overall wellness in Traditional Chinese Medicine. Herbal formulas can help regulate gut function, ease bloating, cramping, and discomfort, and support conditions such as IBS, reflux, and poor appetite. Our practitioners assess your individual presentation to create a formula that addresses the root cause, not just the symptoms.
- **Women's Health & Hormonal Balance** — Herbal medicine is particularly powerful in supporting women's health throughout every life stage. From regulating menstrual cycles and supporting fertility to managing symptoms of perimenopause and menopause, our personalised herbal prescriptions work with your body's natural rhythms to restore hormonal balance and promote lasting wellbeing.

## Practitioners

### Nina Hu — Osteopath & Medical Acupuncture

Profile: https://meridianosteopathy.co.nz/team/nina-hu/

Qualifications: Nina has a Bachelor of Musculoskeletal Health and a Postgraduate Diploma in Osteopathy. She has also completed training in Postgraduate Certificate in Health reputation for successful treatment outcomes in clinic.

Approach: During Nina's childhood, she experienced delayed speech development, a challenge including energy healing, Naturopathy, and Acupuncture to address this issue. Despite not being excel in verbal, intuitive feeling and palpation.

Special interests: When Nina feels stressed or low energy levels, she does Meditation and Qigong to restore her energy and calm her central nervous system. Serinces with her clients.

About: Nina loves sports and often be found engaging in activities such Badminton, Skiing in the mountains, and Kayaking in the Bay. With her first-hand experience of sports-related injuries, she empathizes with the frustrations they bring and offers custom address them effectively.

### Jonathan Grice — Osteopath & Low Level Laser Therapy

Profile: https://meridianosteopathy.co.nz/team/jonathan-grice/

Qualifications: Jonathan trained at the British College of Osteopathic Medicine in London and is a NZ Registered Osteopath. After graduating, he built extensive clinical experience in private practice from 2010, before holding an NHS contract in Twickenham where he received GP referrals. From 2019 to 2022 he worked in advanced clinical practice at an NHS GP surgery, focusing on musculoskeletal diagnosis and onward referral. Born and raised in Christchurch, Jonathan returned home in 2022 — bringing with him over a decade of international experience, including a period as a vaccinator during the COVID-19 pandemic.

Approach: Jonathan's treatments draw on structural, classical, and cranial osteopathic techniques, tailored to each patient's presentation and goals. Alongside hands-on osteopathy, he incorporates bio-photomodulation (low-level laser therapy) for pain management and tissue healing — an area in which he has developed particular expertise through his work with THOR Photomedicine, where he authored over 200 treatment protocols. He also uses kinesiology taping and exercise rehabilitation where clinically appropriate.

Special interests: Jonathan has a deep specialist interest in bio-photomodulation and its clinical applications in musculoskeletal and chronic pain conditions. His work with THOR Photomedicine has seen him develop protocols for a wide range of presentations — from aphthous ulcers to post-herpetic neuralgia. He also brings extensive experience managing complex musculoskeletal cases from his years working within NHS general practice.

About: Born and raised in Christchurch, Jonathan spent many years working in the UK before returning home in 2022. He is glad to be back in the city he grew up in, and brings a broad clinical perspective shaped by years of NHS practice and international collaboration. Outside the clinic, Jonathan remains connected to his community and enjoys the outdoor lifestyle that Christchurch and the surrounding region have to offer.

### Maddison Moffat — Osteopath

Profile: https://meridianosteopathy.co.nz/team/maddison-moffat/

Qualifications: Maddison completed a Bachelor of Musculoskeletal Health and a Postgraduate Diploma in Osteopathy at Ara Institute of Canterbury. She is registered with the Osteopathic Council of New Zealand and Osteopaths New Zealand, and is an ACC-registered provider. Born and raised in Christchurch, Maddison developed a passion for understanding the human body and movement from an early age through her lifelong involvement in sport.

Approach: Maddison embraces osteopathy's holistic philosophy, providing individualised, patient-centred care that considers the whole body rather than just the site of pain or injury. She works with patients across a broad spectrum — from acute injuries to long-standing chronic conditions — adapting her approach to suit each person's needs, history, and goals. Her aim is always to help people return to the activities they love.

Special interests: Maddison has a particular interest in sports-related injuries and movement-based complaints, informed by her own active lifestyle and lifelong connection to sport. She also has a genuine enthusiasm for working with children, bringing both care and patience to paediatric appointments. Whether supporting an athlete through recovery or helping a patient manage ongoing pain, Maddison brings the same commitment and warmth to every consultation.

About: Outside the clinic, Maddison lives an active life — you'll find her at the gym, out walking, or cheering on her favourite sports teams. A proud Christchurch local, she loves being part of a community-focused practice where she can make a real difference to people's everyday lives. She believes that a strong therapeutic relationship is the foundation of good care, and works hard to make every patient feel genuinely supported.

### Min Jin — Acupuncturist

Profile: https://meridianosteopathy.co.nz/team/min-jin/

Qualifications: Min holds a Bachelor of Clinical Medicine from Gansu University of Chinese Medicine (2002) and a Master of Biomedical Project Management from the University of Science and Technology of China (2013). In 2025, she completed a Bachelor's degree at the New Zealand School of Acupuncture and Traditional Chinese Medicine, which included over 320 hours of supervised clinical practice. Min is a registered member of the Chinese Medicine Council of New Zealand (CMCNZ). Before establishing her acupuncture practice in New Zealand, Min spent many years in clinical research — including four years at Peking University Stem Cell Research Centre and over a decade managing nationally approved clinical projects in areas such as Parkinson's disease, spinal cord injury, and oncology.

Approach: Min provides safe, evidence-informed, and patient-centred care, weaving together classical Chinese medicine theory with Tung-style acupuncture techniques. She takes time to understand each patient's full picture — physical, emotional, and lifestyle — before forming a treatment plan. Alongside acupuncture, she draws on moxibustion, cupping, and therapeutic massage where clinically appropriate, tailoring every session to the individual.

Special interests: Min's clinical focus includes musculoskeletal pain (neck, shoulder, and back), headaches and migraines, digestive complaints, sleep difficulties, stress-related symptoms, and women's health — including menstrual irregularities and menopausal symptoms. Her background in clinical research gives her a deep appreciation for evidence-based practice, which she brings together with the wisdom of traditional Chinese medicine to offer a uniquely informed approach to care.

About: Min's path to acupuncture is shaped by a rich background in both science and medicine. Her years in clinical research gave her a profound respect for evidence-based thinking, which she combines with a genuine belief in the healing potential of traditional Chinese medicine. Outside of work, Min values balance — she enjoys time in nature, nourishing food, and the quieter rhythms of everyday life. She brings patience, warmth, and genuine curiosity to every consultation.

## Consultation Process

1. **Initial Consultation** — Consent form, case history, physical examination, treatment plan discussion. 45–60 minutes. Gentle hands-on treatment including balanced ligamentous tension, soft tissue techniques, and myofascial unwinding.
2. **Follow-up Treatments** — Tailored to your response. May include different osteopathic techniques, dry needling, auricular/electrical acupuncture, and cupping based on progress.
3. **Rehabilitation** — Personalised rehab via Physitrack app — exercise videos sent to your phone. Designed to prevent future injuries and support long-term recovery.
